description: This file defines affirmative sentences in the Future Perfect tense in Njo Viri (Balanda). The future perfect tense expresses actions that will be completed before a specific point in the future.

general_principle.structure: Future perfect affirmative sentences in Balanda are formed by attaching the future prefix 'le-' to the base verb and adding the result marker 'dheki' at the end of the clause.

affirmative_structure.notes: Used to express that an action will have been completed in the future. | The future prefix 'le-' must attach directly to the verb base; The verb must remain in base form; The result marker 'dheki' must appear sentence-final; Objects and complements must appear before 'dheki'

tense_and_aspect_distinction.structure: Future perfect encodes future tense and completion.
tense_and_aspect_distinction.notes: 'le-' encodes future tense; 'dheki' encodes completed result

error_constraints.structure: Do not omit 'le-'; Do not omit 'dheki'; Do not use the progressive prefix 'ti-'; Do not use the copula 'ma'; Do not add the negation marker 'gba' in affirmatives; Do not move 'dheki' from sentence-final position

output: I will have eaten fish
She will have drunk water
We will have slept on the bed
Uku will have gone
Ngande will have come home
They will have walked in the compound
She will have run fast
Uku will have worked in the garden
The child will have played in the compound
I will have watched the dog
I will have spoken Balanda
The girl will have written words
I will have read a book
He will have thought about it
They will have known it
I will have asked them
Ngande will have told them

input: Wilii lekeye ti naa ni dheki
output: The girl will have called her mother
I will have loved you

She will have hated him
Ngande will have surprised him
He will have seemed calm
The girl will have tied the shoe
He will have cut the pot
I will have pulled the door
We will have pushed the box
The girl will have washed the shoe
I will have hidden the beads
Ngande will have built a house
I will have created words
She will have changed the words
I will have turned the door
We will have added money
I will have described Balanda
They will have helped the poor
Ngande will have initiated the project
I will have contributed to the project
The girl will have separated trash
We will have upheld the law
I will have wanted water
She will have given money
I will have found money
Uku will have tried the cloth
Ngande will have left home
I will have arrived in Wau
He will have sharpened the knife
Ngande will have imitated adults
We will have started the project
He will have fallen
